data.name: Goryu (JP Victory)
data.description: Red and black Dinoforce leader encased in a Tyrannosaurus Pretender shell with massive jaws and clawed arms. Inner robot transforms from the dinosaur shell.

data.name: Kakuryu (JP Victory)
data.description: Blue Dinoforce member wearing a triceratops Pretender shell with a horned frill and stocky quadruped build. Inner robot transforms from the dinosaur shell.

data.name: Rairyu (JP Victory)
data.description: Green Dinoforce member encased in a long-necked brontosaurus Pretender shell with a towering profile. Inner robot transforms from the dinosaur shell.

data.name: Doryu (JP Victory)
data.description: Purple Dinoforce member wearing a stegosaurus Pretender shell with prominent back plates and a spiked tail. Inner robot transforms from the dinosaur shell.

data.name: Gairyu (JP Victory)
data.description: Yellow Dinoforce member encased in a pteranodon Pretender shell with wide leathery wings and a crested head. Inner robot transforms from the dinosaur shell.

data.name: Yokuryu (JP Victory)
data.description: Teal Dinoforce member wearing a plesiosaur Pretender shell with a long serpentine neck and flippered limbs. Inner robot transforms from the dinosaur shell.

data.name: Dinoking (JP Victory)
data.description: Combined form of all six Dinoforce Pretenders. A hulking gestalt assembled from dinosaur-shelled warriors, with Goryu's T-rex torso dominating the center and reptilian limbs from the remaining five members.

data.name: Jan Minakaze (JP Victory)
data.description: Young human boy with brown hair and an energetic personality, serving as Star Saber's partner and adopted son. No alt mode.

data.name: Illumina (JP Victory)
data.description: Female human scientist and teacher with long hair, acting as a caretaker and ally to the Autobots. No alt mode.

data.name: Deathcobra (JP Victory)
data.description: Purple Decepticon jet warrior with a sharp, angular frame and cobra-themed head crest. Transforms into a purple jet.

data.name: Dai Atlas (JP Zone)
data.description: Blue and white Powered Master Supreme Commander with a tall, broad-shouldered frame and drill-tipped forearm. Triple-changer that transforms into a drill tank, a jet, and a base mode.

data.name: Sonic Bomber (JP Zone)
data.description: Red and white Powered Master with a bulky bomber-jet fuselage chest and twin engine nacelles on the shoulders. Transforms into a bomber jet and a base mode.

data.name: Sky Powered (JP Zone)
data.description: Combined form of Dai Atlas and Sonic Bomber. A massive winged warrior with Dai Atlas as the main body and Sonic Bomber forming the wing and booster pack, creating an enormous aerial-capable super robot.

data.name: Roadfire (JP Zone)
data.description: Red Powered Master with a sturdy, firefighter-styled frame and ladder rack details across the back. Transforms into a fire truck and a base mode.

data.name: Rabbicrater (JP Zone)
data.description: White Micromaster with a rounded lunar-exploration design and oversized wheels on the limbs. Transforms into a compact lunar rover.

data.name: Moonradar (JP Zone)
data.description: Black Micromaster with a sleek sports car silhouette and radar dish details on the back. Transforms into a black Porsche.

data.name: Speeder (JP Zone)
data.description: Red Micromaster with an aerodynamic racing-styled frame and a spoiler on the back. Transforms into a red race car.

data.name: Sonic (JP Zone)
data.description: Blue Micromaster with swept-back jet wings and a compact aerial fighter build. Transforms into a blue jet.

data.name: Violengiguar (JP Zone)
data.description: Dark purple and green three-faced insectoid energy being with a grotesque, segmented body that splits into the three creatures Vion, Lenja, and Guar. No conventional alt mode.

data.name: Metrotitan (JP Zone)
data.description: Black and purple Decepticon city-former of colossal scale, rivaling Metroplex in size with fortress towers on the shoulders. Transforms into a city and a battle station.

data.name: Cain (JP Zone)
data.description: Small blue alien creature native to the Zone, with large expressive eyes and a diminutive stature. No alt mode.

data.name: Emusa (JP Zone)
data.description: Small green alien creature native to the Zone, with a gentle appearance and tiny frame. No alt mode.

data.name: Akira (JP Zone)
data.description: Young human boy allied with Dai Atlas, with dark hair and a brave personality. No alt mode.

data.name: Ratchet (G1 Toy)
data.description: White and red Autobot with no humanoid head — robot mode is a flat van-roof platform with a sticker face on the windshield area. Includes a medical bay accessory platform. Transforms into a white Nissan Onebox Vanette ambulance with red cross markings

data.name: Ironhide (G1 Toy)
data.description: Red Autobot with no humanoid head — robot mode is a flat van-roof platform with a sticker face, identical mold to Ratchet in red. Includes a battle sled accessory platform. Transforms into a red Nissan Onebox Vanette

data.name: Jetfire (G1 Toy)
data.description: White and red Autobot fighter jet based on the Bandai Macross VF-1S Super Valkyrie mold. Tall robot with FAST Pack armor accessories, helmet with gold visor, and wing-mounted boosters. Completely different design from the cartoon character Skyfire. Transforms into a VF-1S Valkyrie variable fighter

data.name: Megatron (G1 Toy)
data.description: Chrome silver Decepticon leader with a realistic Walther P-38 pistol alt mode. Robot mode features the gun barrel as a right-arm fusion cannon, scope mounted on the left arm, and a blocky chrome torso. Noticeably different proportions from the cartoon — stockier with visible gun components throughout the robot mode

data.name: Ultra Magnus (G1 Toy)
data.description: White Autobot cab-robot (inner robot is a white repaint of the Optimus Prime mold) with snap-on blue, red, and white car carrier armor that forms the larger super robot mode. Unlike the cartoon which only shows the armored form, the toy reveals a distinct white Prime underneath. Transforms into a white cab-over semi-truck with car carrier trailer

data.name: Swoop (G1 Toy)
data.description: Blue and red Autobot Dinobot pteranodon — dramatically different from the cartoon’s red and gold color scheme. Toy robot mode has a blue chest with red wings and chrome gold sword. Transforms into a mechanical pteranodon in blue and red

data.name: Bluestreak (G1 Toy)
data.description: Silver and black Autobot — despite the name suggesting blue, the released toy is entirely silver/gray with black hood details and red Autobot insignia. The pre-production prototype was blue (matching the name), but all retail releases use the silver Datsun 280ZX Turbo deco. Transforms into a silver Datsun 280ZX Turbo with roof-mounted rocket launchers

data.name: Galvatron (G1 Toy)
data.description: Gray, orange, and purple Decepticon leader — strikingly different from the cartoon’s predominantly purple design. Toy robot mode is mostly light gray with an orange cannon arm and purple accents. Electronics feature light-up cannon barrel and sound effects. Transforms into a futuristic laser cannon

data.name: Astrotrain (G1 Toy US)
data.description: White, gray, and purple Decepticon Triple Changer — the US toy release has a predominantly white shuttle body with purple and gray accents, distinctly lighter than the cartoon’s deep purple. Transforms into a white space shuttle and a gray/white locomotive

data.name: Astrotrain (G1 Toy JP)
data.description: Black and purple Decepticon Triple Changer — the Japanese toy release features a striking black shuttle body with purple accents, dramatically different from both the US white release and the cartoon’s purple design. Transforms into a black space shuttle and a black/purple locomotive

data.name: Shockwave (G1 Toy RadioShack)
data.description: Gray Decepticon laser gun robot — the RadioShack "Galactic Man" release is predominantly gray/silver rather than the cartoon’s light purple/lavender. Features working electronics with light-up barrel and sound effects. Transforms into a large gray laser pistol

data.name: Optimus Prime (G2 Toy)
data.description: Red cab similar to G1 but with a black trailer replacing the original gray, plus added missile launchers, electronic sound box, and new G2 Autobot logos. Transforms into a cab-over semi-truck with black trailer.

data.name: Jazz (G2 Toy)
data.description: White body with blue and green accents in a rearranged color layout from G1's white/blue/red scheme. G1 Porsche 935 mold. Transforms into a white Porsche 935.

data.name: Sideswipe (G2 Toy)
data.description: Black body with red bumper — reversed from G1's red body with black accents — and neon green racing decals. G1 Lamborghini Countach mold. Transforms into a black Lamborghini Countach.

data.name: Inferno (G2 Toy)
data.description: Red fire engine largely similar to G1 but with new G2 stickers and an added water squirter accessory. Transforms into a red Mitsubishi Fuso fire engine.

data.name: Starscream (G2 Toy)
data.description: Off-white body with camouflage deco, salmon/pink torso, purple accents, and neon highlights — dramatically different from G1's gray/red/blue. G1 F-15 Seeker mold. Transforms into a camouflage F-15 Eagle.

data.name: Ramjet (G2 Toy)
data.description: Bright purple body with teal/turquoise detailing and black accents — a bold departure from G1's white with red and black. G1 Conehead Seeker mold. Transforms into a purple F-15 Eagle with swept-forward wings.

data.name: Grimlock (G2 Toy)
data.description: Turquoise/blue robot — the most iconic G2 Dinobot deco, replacing G1's silver/gray color scheme. Transforms into a turquoise mechanical Tyrannosaurus rex.

data.name: Slag (G2 Toy)
data.description: Green body — a bold departure from G1's red/gray scheme. Part of the G2 Dinobots color-swapped reissues. Transforms into a green mechanical triceratops.

data.name: Snarl (G2 Toy)
data.description: Red body replacing G1's gold/yellow deco. Part of the G2 Dinobots color-swapped reissues. Transforms into a red mechanical stegosaurus.

data.name: Bumblebee (G2 Toy)
data.description: Vacuum-metallized gold chrome replacing G1's matte yellow, with an eye-catching metallic finish. G1 VW Beetle mold. Transforms into a gold chrome Volkswagen Beetle.

data.name: Hubcap (G2 Toy)
data.description: Metallic candy red chrome with orange face accents — a flashy departure from G1's yellow. G1 Bumblebee retool mold. Transforms into a candy red chrome Volkswagen Beetle.

data.name: Beachcomber (G2 Toy)
data.description: Emerald green chrome with dark gray and neon orange-red highlights, replacing G1's blue/teal. G1 dune buggy mold. Transforms into an emerald green chrome dune buggy.

data.name: Seaspray (G2 Toy)
data.description: Vacuum-metallized blue, white, and yellow chrome — closest to G1 colors among the G2 Minibots but with a flashy metallic finish. G1 hovercraft mold. Transforms into a chrome hovercraft.

data.name: Scrapper (G2 Toy)
data.description: Yellow Constructicon with lighter purple accents, replacing G1's green/purple scheme. G2 Decepticon tampographs. Transforms into a yellow front-end loader.

data.name: Hook (G2 Toy)
data.description: Yellow Constructicon with lighter purple accents, replacing G1's green/purple scheme. G2 Decepticon tampographs. Transforms into a yellow crane truck.

data.name: Bonecrusher (G2 Toy)
data.description: Yellow Constructicon with lighter purple accents, replacing G1's green/purple scheme. G2 Decepticon tampographs. Transforms into a yellow bulldozer.

data.name: Long Haul (G2 Toy)
data.description: Yellow Constructicon with lighter purple accents, replacing G1's green/purple scheme. G2 Decepticon tampographs. Transforms into a yellow dump truck.

data.name: Scavenger (G2 Toy)
data.description: Yellow Constructicon with lighter purple accents, replacing G1's green/purple scheme. G2 Decepticon tampographs. Transforms into a yellow excavator.

data.name: Mixmaster (G2 Toy)
data.description: Yellow Constructicon with lighter purple accents, replacing G1's green/purple scheme. G2 Decepticon tampographs. Transforms into a yellow cement mixer.

data.name: Devastator (G2 Toy)
data.description: Massive yellow and purple combined form of the six G2 Constructicons, replacing G1's green/purple. Same imposing silhouette but in a striking yellow color scheme.

data.name: Silverbolt (G2 Toy)
data.description: Light blue and silver with orange highlights, replacing G1's white/red scheme. Part of the G2 Aerialbots with bold decorative stickers. Transforms into a light blue Concorde SST.

data.name: Air Raid (G2 Toy)
data.description: Blue with new pattern stickers — brighter than G1's black scheme. Part of the G2 Aerialbots with bold decorative stickers. Transforms into a blue F-15 Eagle.

data.name: Skydive (G2 Toy)
data.description: Silver and blue with checkered flag patterns, replacing G1's gray/red scheme. Part of the G2 Aerialbots with bold decorative stickers. Transforms into a silver F-16 Fighting Falcon.

data.name: Fireflight (G2 Toy)
data.description: Dark red with flame stickers, replacing G1's white/red scheme. Part of the G2 Aerialbots with bold decorative stickers. Transforms into a dark red F-4 Phantom.

data.name: Slingshot (G2 Toy)
data.description: Swirly metallic gold plastic replaces G1's white, a dramatic departure in finish and color. Transforms into a gold AV-8B Harrier II jump jet as part of the G2 Aerialbots.

data.name: Superion (G2 Toy)
data.description: Combined form of the G2 Aerialbots — a striking multicolored patchwork of blue, silver, gold, and red replacing G1's clean white and red. The chaotic color mix gives the gestalt a uniquely aggressive appearance.

data.name: Onslaught (G2 Toy)
data.description: Orange and yellow body with purple camouflage splotches replacing G1's military blue. Transforms into a yellow anti-aircraft truck sporting the signature G2 Combaticon camo pattern.

data.name: Brawl (G2 Toy)
data.description: Bright green with purple camouflage splotches — a different, more vivid green than G1's military drab with added camo detail. Transforms into a green Leopard 1 tank covered in purple camo.

data.name: Swindle (G2 Toy)
data.description: Bright red with purple camouflage splotches replacing G1's tan and brown earth tones. Transforms into a red FMC XR311 combat support vehicle with purple camo accents.

data.name: Blast Off (G2 Toy)
data.description: White and black with purple camouflage splotches replacing G1's brown and tan color scheme. Transforms into a white space shuttle marked with the G2 Combaticon camo pattern.

data.name: Vortex (G2 Toy)
data.description: Blue with purple camouflage splotches replacing G1's gray military finish. Transforms into a blue UH-60 Black Hawk helicopter decorated with the unified G2 Combaticon camo.

data.name: Bruticus (G2 Toy)
data.description: Multicolored combined form of the G2 Combaticons with purple camouflage throughout — orange, green, red, white, and blue limbs create a wildly colorful departure from G1's subdued military drab. The unified camo pattern ties the chaotic palette together.

data.name: Motormaster (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Black with red accents and purple details — a darker scheme than G1's gray and purple, though only unpainted prototypes have surfaced. Transforms into a black Kenworth K100 semi-truck.

data.name: Dead End (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Candy-apple red replacing G1's maroon, with fewer than 12 carded samples known to exist. Transforms into a red Porsche 928.

data.name: Drag Strip (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Gold, blue, and purple with a checkerboard pattern on the spoiler — a flamboyant departure from G1's plain yellow. Transforms into a gold Tyrrell P34 six-wheeled race car.

data.name: Wildrider (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Bright yellow replacing G1's gray and red, with fewer than 12 carded samples known to exist. Transforms into a yellow Ferrari 308 GTB.

data.name: Breakdown (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Teal blue body with purple hood and skirt stripes replacing G1's white and blue. Approximately 300 were produced as a BotCon 1994 exclusive, making it the only G2 Stunticon to reach any distribution. Transforms into a teal Lamborghini Countach.

data.name: Menasor (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Combined form of the G2 Stunticons — a riot of neon colors including black, red, gold, yellow, and teal replacing G1's gray and purple. Never produced beyond test shots.

data.name: Hot Spot (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Exact colors are poorly documented — known to exist only in prototype form, never in finished packaging, making it the rarest of the G2 Protectobots. Transforms into a fire engine.

data.name: First Aid (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. White and blue body with teal chest and gold head — a bold departure from G1's white and red emergency colors. Transforms into a white Toyota Hiace ambulance.

data.name: Groove (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Neon orange and teal replacing G1's white and gold police motorcycle colors. Transforms into an orange and teal Honda Gold Wing motorcycle.

data.name: Streetwise (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Black with blue windows replacing G1's white police car livery. Transforms into a black Nissan 300ZX police car.

data.name: Blades (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Bold non-G1 color scheme — exact colors are poorly documented due to extreme rarity of surviving samples. Transforms into a helicopter.

data.name: Defensor (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Combined form of the G2 Protectobots — described as having one of the era's more outlandish color schemes, with fewer than 6 samples of each component known to exist.

data.name: Sludge (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Orange replacing G1's gray and gold — never produced for any G2 retail wave. Transforms into an orange mechanical Brontosaurus.

data.name: Swoop (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Bright green replacing G1's blue and red toy colors — never produced for any G2 retail wave. Transforms into a green mechanical pteranodon.

data.name: Mirage (G2 Toy)
data.description: Unreleased prototype. Green and purple replacing G1's blue and white Ligier livery. Transforms into a green and purple Ligier JS11 Formula One race car.
